label: Scatter Utility
description: Scatters entity IDs to the next workflow step OR invokes a target klados directly when target_klados is specified (generic scatter mode).
detailed_description:
## Overview

The Scatter Utility is a versatile klados for workflow orchestration. It accepts multiple entities via `target_entities` and fans them out to the next workflow step.

## Two Modes of Operation

### 1. Rhiza Handoff Mode (default)

Receives entity IDs via `target_entities` and returns them as outputs for rhiza's scatter handoff to dispatch in parallel.

**Invocation:**
```json
POST /rhizai/{id}/invoke
{
  "target_collection": "...",
  "target_entities": ["ENTITY_001", "ENTITY_002", "ENTITY_003"],
  "confirm": true
}
```

### 2. Generic Scatter Mode

When `input.target_klados` or `input.target_rhiza` is specified, invokes the target directly for each entity ID (fire-and-forget).

**Invocation:**
```json
POST /rhizai/{id}/invoke
{
  "target_collection": "...",
  "target_entities": ["e1", "e2", "e3"],
  "input": {
    "target_klados": "DESCRIBE_KLADOS_ID",
    "passthrough_input": { "mode": "detailed" }
  },
  "confirm": true
}
```

**Generic Mode Features:**
- ≤50 items: Direct invocation with concurrency control (10 parallel)
- >50 items: Delegates to scatter-utility service for batched dispatch
- Fire-and-forget: No result collection (use rhiza for gather)
- `passthrough_input`: Optional input passed to each target invocation

## When to Use

- **Rhiza mode**: Workflow entry point that processes multiple entities in parallel
- **Generic mode**: AI agents that need to scatter entities to a target without creating a workflow

## Backward Compatibility

The klados also supports the deprecated `input.entity_ids` format for existing workflows. New workflows should use `target_entities`.

## Limitations

- Does not validate that entity IDs actually exist (validation happens downstream)
- Generic mode is fire-and-forget only (no gather/result collection)
- Cannot filter or transform entities (use a dedicated klados for that)

klados_input.description: Optional parameters for generic scatter mode (invoking a target directly).

target_klados.description: Klados ID to invoke for each entity (generic scatter mode). When specified, invokes target directly instead of returning outputs for rhiza handoff.

target_rhiza.description: Rhiza ID to invoke for each entity (generic scatter mode). Alternative to target_klados.

passthrough_input.description: Input data to pass to each target invocation (only used in generic scatter mode).

entity_ids.description: DEPRECATED: Use target_entities in the invoke request instead. Kept for backward compatibility.

handoff.outputs: All entity IDs from input.entity_ids, normalized to Output format
handoff.description: Returns all input entity IDs as outputs. When used with a scatter handoff (e.g., { scatter: "next_step" }), rhiza dispatches each ID to the next step in parallel. The Output format preserves any routing properties passed in the input.

processing.tier_description: Simple Tier 1 worker using KladosJob with waitUntil(). No Durable Objects needed since processing is instantaneous.
processing.idempotent_description: Running with the same input always produces the same outputs. No side effects beyond log creation.

size_limits.entity_ids_array: Practical limit around 10,000 IDs per invocation (constrained by request/response size)
size_limits.notes: For very large scatters (>50 items), rhiza automatically uses batched dispatch via scatter-utility service

constraints.rate_limits: No external API calls, limited only by Cloudflare worker limits

side_effects: Creates klados_log entity in job_collection (standard for all kladoi)
In generic scatter mode: invokes target klados/rhiza for each entity ID
No entities created or modified in target_collection (directly)

examples.name: Basic scatter via rhiza invoke
examples.description: Scatter three entities to parallel processing via workflow invocation

workflow_context.notes: Each of CHUNK_001, CHUNK_002, CHUNK_003 will be dispatched to extract step in parallel

examples.name: Generic scatter mode - invoke target directly
examples.description: Scatter entities directly to a klados without a full rhiza workflow
examples.notes: In generic mode, the scatter klados invokes the target directly for each entity. Returns empty outputs (no rhiza handoff needed). passthrough_input is passed to each target invocation.

examples.name: Error case - missing target_entities
examples.description: What happens when target_entities is not provided

output.error_message: target_entities is required (or input.entity_ids for backward compatibility)
